sourceText: Stabilized the frontiers through a combination of military reforms, strategic alliances (including with the Cumans), and shrewd diplomacy. He leveraged the unexpected force of the First Crusade to reclaim key territories in Anatolia, initiating the Komnenian restoration.
What is needed in the most desperate moment is not flashy pride, but the cold courage to compromise in order to survive.
It means the realistic wisdom of overcoming a crisis by negotiating with external forces while enduring humiliation amidst a national crisis.
Patient effort to fill the gaps and stand up cracked pillars extends the lifespan of a crumbling empire.
It shows the patience of taking over a country on the verge of collapse from enemies on all sides and overcoming crises step by step.
Sometimes one hoists internal sails by borrowing a great external wind, but it is governance to steer so that the wind does not swallow the ship.
It refers to the insight of struggling not to lose the initiative while preventing immediate threats by drawing in foreign armies.
